feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Concur
feed.subtitle: SAP Concur blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: Revolutionizing Expense Management: SAP Concur App for Microsoft Teams
entry.content:
We are thrilled to announce the launch of the SAP Concur App for Microsoft Teams, now available in the Microsoft App Store. This powerful integration brings expense management directly into the flow of work, providing employees and approvers with seamless collaboration and enhanced productivity. Here’s a closer look at how this integration is transforming expense management workflows.

Key Features of the SAP Concur App for Microsoft Teams:

  1. View, Share, and Approve Expense Reports: Employees can easily view their most recent active expense reports within Microsoft Teams. The list includes key information such as the status of each report. With just one click, employees can access detailed views of their expense reports in SAP Concur, making the process efficient and user-friendly.

    Sharing expense reports with colleagues or approvers has never been easier. Employees can generate a shareable card for any expense report and forward it to a colleague directly within Teams. The recipient, provided they have the necessary permissions and the SAP Concur App enabled, can view the report and take appropriate action.

    Approvers can quickly access their most recent expense reports awaiting approval through the approval queue in Teams. They can review the details, add comments, and approve or send back reports directly within Teams. All comments and actions are tracked in the audit log in SAP Concur, ensuring transparency and compliance.

  2. Streamlined Employee Actions: Employees can view their expense reports and share them without needing to leave Microsoft Teams, allowing them to stay focused and productive within their workflow.

  3. Simplified Approvals: Approvers save time by reviewing and approving expense reports directly in Teams, with no need to switch applications. This integrated process ensures a smooth and efficient workflow.

How It Works:

  • Sign-In and Connect: Once the SAP Concur App is enabled in Teams, users can sign in and connect their Microsoft Teams account with SAP Concur. This ensures that employees can securely retrieve information from SAP Concur.

  • Intuitive Prompts: Users can interact with the app by typing prompts or selecting actions from a list of available prompts. Whether it’s viewing reports, sharing them, or approving them, the app provides clear and simple workflows.

Prerequisites for Using the SAP Concur for Microsoft Teams App:

To use the SAP Concur for Microsoft Teams app seamlessly, your organization must:

  • Have a contract with SAP Concur and have the Concur Travel and/or Concur Expense solution enabled.

  • Be eligible to and opted into the new Concur Expense and its new booking experience.

  • Get an Administrator to activate the app within your Microsoft environment.

For more information on how to get started, check the SAP Concur help portal for Microsoft Teams.

The Value of Integration:

The SAP Concur App for Microsoft Teams eliminates the need to switch between applications, saving time and improving focus. By bringing expense management into the Teams environment, employees and approvers can:

  • Improve efficiency by managing expenses within their existing collaboration tools.

  • Enhance transparency with easy sharing and tracking of expense reports.

  • Boost productivity by reducing friction in the expense approval process.

This integration is a game-changer for organizations looking to modernize their expense management practices and empower employees with tools that adapt to their workflows.

Ready to Get Started?

The SAP Concur App for Microsoft Teams is now available in the Microsoft App Store. Enable it today and experience the future of seamless expense management. Together, let’s make expense management smarter, faster, and more collaborative.

entry.title: Extending Journal Content and Structure with SAP Concur Expense Data
entry.content:
Travel-to-reimburse:

Extending Journal Content and Structure with SAP Concur Expense Data (Use case: Extending the Journal Entry with the vendor description)

In today's fast-paced business environment, integrating and extending financial data across platforms is crucial for maintaining accurate and comprehensive records and extending the capability of SAP S/4HANA to meet unique business needs which can sometimes be challenging.

When SAP Financial systems receive expense reports from SAP Concur, only standard and limited fields are passed to the journal document.

To improve data tracking and for Reporting purposes, some customers need to customize and add additional Expense Data to the Journal document.  The following passages outline a process to make such enhancements possible.

This blog explains how to extend journal content and structure using the mapping of custom fields between both systems: SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud and SAP Concur

 

Use Case 1: Adding vendor description to the Journal Entry Structure.

This blog post outlines one of business requirements on how to enhance the journal content and structure by incorporating Concur Expense data into SAP S/4 HANA public Cloud edition by adding the vendor description in the journal entry to offer several benefits:

  • Simplifies Reconciliation: Provides context for payments and liabilities.
  • Speeds Up Audit Process: Facilitates quicker audits by providing detailed transaction information.
  • Enhances Internal Controls: Reduces fraud risks such as duplicate payments.

 

To extend the financial Journal Entry with vendor description maintained in the expense report, follow these steps:

1. Create a Custom Field (Vendor Description) in S/4 HANA Public Cloud.

Open the “Custom Fields and Logic” App and Create a New Custom Field:

  • Click on "Create".
  • Select the Business Context relevant to where you want the custom field to be (Journal Entry).

Define the Custom Field:

  • Enter a Label and Name for the custom field.
  • Choose the Type of field (e.g., Text, Number, Date).
  • Set the Length and other properties as required.

 

Enable Field Usage:

  • In the UIs and Reports tab, enable the field for the relevant applications and reports where you want it to appear.
  • You can also enable the field for OData Services if needed.

Save and Publish:

  • Click "Save" to save your custom field.
  • Click "Publish" to make the field available in the selected business context.

 For more information about the creation of the custom field in SAP S/4 Hana Public Cloud,

see Editing Custom Fields.

2. Create a Substitution Rule:

use the "Manage Substitution and Validation Rules" app to map the custom field to the vendor description:

Open the Fiori App:

  • Navigate to the Fiori launchpad and open the "Manage Substitution and Validation Rules" app.

Create a New Rule:

  • Click on "Create Rule".
  • Enter the Business Context (Expense Report: Line Item Level).
  • Select the Event (Expense Report: Line Item Level).
  • Choose Substitution Rule as the Rule Type.
  • Click on "Create".

 Define General Information:

  • Enter a Rule Name and Description.

Set Preconditions (if any): Optional

  • Define any preconditions that must be met for the substitution to occur. This can include specific field values or conditions.

Define Substitution Details:

  • Under the Substitution section, specify the Substitute Type (Substitute with field).
  • Enter the Target Field: the field created in step 1.
  • Enter the Source Field: vendor description.

 

Save and Activate:

  • Click on "Save" to save the rule.
  • Click on "Activate" to make the rule active.

 

3. Creating an expense Report in Concur with vendor details:

This is an example of an Expense report with vendors data.

 

 4. Posting the Expense Report:

Import the document manually or waiting for the automatic import, the posting results to the bellow extended Journal entry, adding the vendor description in the structure.  

 

 Note: the vendor description should only be displayed in the Debit Line Item, and if you have the requirement to clear the vendor on the credit line item, an Implementation of custom logic (BAdI) is needed.

             

Conclusion

Integrating Concur Expense data into S/4 HANA's journal entries enhances financial transparency and control. By following the outlined steps, businesses can achieve a more detailed and accurate financial reporting system.

Stay tuned for more blog posts that will showcase additional use cases covering other business requirements needed to extend the structure and the content of the Journal Entry with SAP Concur expense data.

We always welcome feedback for our SAP Concur Integration (ICS) so if you have any topics that you think would make for an interesting and beneficial blog, then please feel free to send your blog suggestions to our team inbox: sapconcurintegartion@sap.com

entry.title: Concur Quick Expense v4 API
entry.content:
I am a Technical Consultant with SAP Concur and work with customers that wish to interface their data with Concur Expense and Concur Invoice. I regularly collaborate with customers that use APIs to manage the data exchange between their systems and the Expense solution provided by SAP Concur.

The Quick Expense API facilitates the creation of an expense entry by capturing essential details, including date, amount, and type of expense, with the option to attach a receipt image. This new entry can then be added to an expense report within Concur Expense, enabling the user to provide further specifics such as attendees and itemizations.

The Quick Expense API can be useful in many scenarios. Transactions can be created to aid in testing cycles, easing the burden on your testers to manually create expense entries. If you have a card program that is not able to be automatically imported into Concur from the card provider, you can automate the import of these transactions from information provided to you by the provider. The time savings from being able to programmatically create these transactions can be a significant savings for your organization.

Prerequisites

This post will not discuss authentication with Concur Expense. Please see this post to get an explanation on authentication and application building with Concur Expense.

You will need an application with the following SCOPEs.

Required Scopes:

  • quickexpense.writeonly          - Write quick expense
  • user.read                                - Get User Information, necessary for userID if using a user token
  • receipts.writeonly                   - Required if e-Bunsho is enabled

 

Optional Scope:

  • CONFIG                                 - Get Expense Configuration info, necessary for expenseTypeId

 

Works with these SAP Concur solutions:

  • Expense – Standard
  • Expense – Professional
  • Japan Market: If the partner is using this API to provide the e-Bunsho digital timestamp for the receipt, the partner should confirm that the client has e-Bunsho activated in SAP Concur

Introduction

The Quick Expense API will be discussed with examples for creating the transactions with and without an image. The images shown are tested using Opensource client API Bruno v.1.34.2. You may need to adjust the payload/configuration based upon the API client used.

Additional details regarding the Quick Expense v4 API can be found here.

Detailed Walkthrough

Determine the userID for the Quick Expense

The first step you should complete is to determine the userID that the transaction will be assigned. You can retrieve the profile of the user using this Profile API. This is the reason the user.read scope is listed as required for the Quick Expense API. This API could be used to determine the userID of the user making the profile API call.

GET     https://{datacenterURI}/profile/v1/me?schema=compact

If you wish to determine the userID for the given user by calling another Concur API, there are many to choose from that can be found in the Developer center documentation. These may require additional scopes to be added to the application. One example would be the use of the Identity v4.0 API.

Once the userID is determined, the expenseTypeId will need to be found.

Determine the expenseTypeId for the Quick Expense

The next step that must be completed is to determine the policies that the user is assigned. Transactions can only be created for expense types assigned to policies the user has been given. So, you must first determine the policies that the user has been assigned. Once you have identified the policies the user has been assigned, then you can determine the expense types available for the transaction which will be created by the Quick Expense API. If the Quick Expense API is not supplied an expense type, or a transaction is assigned an expense type that is not allowed for the user, the API will fail.

Determine the policies that are assigned for the user. The userID will be the value found in the last step.

GET     https://{datacenterURI}/expenseconfig/v4/users/{userID}/policies

Additional details regarding the Expense Configuration v4 APIs can be found here.

Example of the policy results for a given userID:

Next, determine the list of expense types available to a user based on a particular policy found in the previous API call. Based on the results, one of the expenseTypeId values will be used to create the transaction using the Quick Expense API.

GET     https://{datacenterURI}/expenseconfig/v4/users/{userID}/policies/{policyId}/expensetypes

Additional details regarding the Expense Configuration v4 APIs can be found here.

Example of the expense type results for a given userID and policyId:

Creating a Quick Expense without an Image

You have now determined the userID that the transaction will be assigned as well as the expenseTypeId. The template for this API call is listed below. Additional details can be found here.

Template for Quick Expense v4 API without an Image

POST   https://{datacenterURI}/quickexpense/v4/users/{userID}/context/{contextType}/quickexpenses

 

Name

Type

Description

userID

string

Required: The unique identifier of the SAP Concur user.

contextType

string

Required: The access level of the SAP Concur user, which determines the form fields they can view/modify. Supported value: TRAVELER.

You are now ready to create the payload for the Quick Expense.

First, determine the paymentTypeId that should be used for the quick expense.

Supported values:

  • CASHX  – Cash
  • CPAID   – Company Paid
  • PENDC – Pending Card Transaction

Next, create the body of the payload with the appropriate values that you wish to load for the user. I have an example payload below that you can use as a reference.

{
  "comment": "Team Lunch to Discuss Quick Expense",
  "expenseTypeId": "MEALS",
  "location": {
       "city": "Atlanta",
       "countryCode": "US",
       "countrySubDivisionCode": "US-GA"
  },
  "paymentTypeId": "CASHX",
  "transactionAmount": {
       "currencyCode": "USD",
       "value": 30.99
  },
  "transactionDate": "2024-12-27",
  "vendor": "Subway"
}

Then, call the Quick Expense v4 API with your payload.

Example of the results using the above payload:

This is what the expense will appear as in Concur.

There may be additional details required for the Concur user to enter once the expense is assigned to a report which is dependent upon the Concur configuration.

 

Creating a Quick Expense with an Image

As discussed in the previous section, you have now determined the userID that the transaction will be assigned as well as the expenseTypeId.

The template for this API call is listed below. Additional details can be found here.

Template for Quick Expense v4 API with an Image

POST     https://{datacenterURI}/quickexpense/v4/users/{userID}/context/{contextType}/quickexpenses/image

 

Name

Type

Description

userID

string

Required The unique identifier of the SAP Concur user.

contextType

string

Required The access level of the SAP Concur user, which determines the form fields they can view/modify. Supported values: TRAVELER.

fileContent

file

Required The quick expense image. Maximum size 50 MB. Supported image types are: PNG, PDF, TIFF, JPEG

Creating a quick expense with an image requires a multi-part form.

You can use the same format for the body as the previous example. A key value is added for the body titled “quickExpenseRequest”.

{
  "comment": "Team Lunch to Discuss Quick Expense",
  "expenseTypeId": "MEALS",
  "location": {
       "city": "New York",
       "countryCode": "US",
       "countrySubDivisionCode": "US-NY"
  },
  "paymentTypeId": "CASHX",
  "transactionAmount": {
       "currencyCode": "USD",
       "value": 33.87
  },
  "transactionDate": "2024-12-27",
  "vendor": "Good Restaurant"
}

A key value is also added for the body titled “fileContent”. The image that you wish to load should then be specified.

Example of the results using the above quickExpenseRequest payload and a fileContent image:

This is what the expense will appear as in Concur.

Conclusion

The Quick Expense API can easily be used to create expenses that can be assigned to a user. There can be significant time savings gained by using this API whether it is utilized to create test transactions or create transactions that could not be created by a card vendor.

Share and Connect

What do you think?  Do you have anything to add? Leave a comment below.

Did you find it useful? Give us a like and share on social media.

Want to know more about SAP Concur? Please follow here.

Want to ask questions about SAP Concur and its offerings? Ask here

Follow my profile for similar content.

Thank you!

entry.title: Are you looking for administration and configuration tools to manage Concur Request. Concur Request?
entry.content:
I  recommend to access our Getting Started with Concur Request Professional for Administrators learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey is designed to to introduce you to the common administration and configuration tools, to manage Concur Request. Concur Request is a powerful tool that allows users to document and preauthorize upcoming expenses before they occur. This allows for better budgeting and allocation of funds. Concur Request accommodates both travel and non-travel related expenses.

Learning objectives
Upon completion of this learning journey, administrators will be able to administer and configure Concur Request using common tools such as List Management, Audit Rules, Email Reminders, and more. 

Prerequisites

  • Administrators should have a firm understanding of how Concur Request works as a business user.
    Review the learning journey, Getting Started with Concur Professional Request for Business Users, before completing this learning journey.

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Are you an administrators and want to learn how to work with Invoice Pay in Concur Invoice Standard?
entry.content:
I  recommend to access our Working with Invoice Pay in Concur Invoice Standard learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey, for administrators, focuses on configuring and maintaining payment configurations using Automated Clearing House (ACH), Virtual Cards, or Checks in Concur Invoice Standard with the Invoice Pay service.

Learning objectives
After completing this course, administrators will be able to identify and explain the key features of Concur Invoice Payment Manager including how to configure and maintain ACH, Virtual Cards, and Check payments, to ensure payments are made accurately and on-time to vendors. 

Prerequisites

  • Administrators should have a basic understanding of Concur Invoice and the Procure to Pay process.

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Are you a Business User and want to learn the basics of Concur Request?
entry.content:
I  recommend to access our Getting Started with Concur Standard Request for Business Users learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey is an introduction to Concur Request for Business Users. In this comprehensive journey, you will learn the basics of Concur Request, from creating and working with requests to reviewing and approving them as an approver.

Learning objectives
After completing this course, business users will be able to proficiently manage their requests by using the Concur Request platform to submit, approve, and process requests.

Prerequisites

  • A general understanding of SAP Concur including Concur Expense and Concur Travel.

 Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Are you an administrator and want to getting up and running Concur Invoice Payment Manager?
entry.content:
I  recommend to access our Working with Invoice Pay in Concur Invoice Professional learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey, for administrators, focuses on configuring and maintaining payment configurations using Automated Clearing House (ACH), Virtual Cards, or Checks in Concur Invoice Professional with the Invoice Pay service.

Learning Objectives
After completing this course, administrators will be able to identify and explain the key features of Concur Invoice Payment Manager including how to configure and maintain ACH, Virtual Cards, and Check payments, to ensure payments are made accurately and on-time to vendors. 

Prerequisites

  • Administrators should have a basic understanding of Concur Invoice and the Procure to Pay process.  

 Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Unlocking New Possibilities with the GROW with SAP Premium Package
entry.content:
Last year SAP announced a new packaged solution that bundles various cloud-based solutions:
GROW with SAP. This Blog will focus on the GROW with SAP Premium offering and how it relates to
Spend Management with SAP Concur. 
In today's fast-paced business environment, companies need robust solutions to stay ahead of the curve. With the GROW with SAP Premium package, SAP announced a solution with access to SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Concur Expense. This bundle delivers an embedded integration between SAP Cloud ERP and SAP Concur Expense through SAP ICS. This powerful combination is designed to help organizations streamline their operations, enhance financial visibility, and drive growth. 

What is GROW with SAP? 

GROW with SAP is a comprehensive cloud ERP solution tailored for fast-growing organizations. It consists of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition targeting midmarket businesses by offering a complete suite of solutions, services, and learning resources to help companies successfully adopt cloud ERP.  

What is GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Premium? 

GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, premium enriches SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with features that accelerate development and automation and capabilities for procurement and advanced financial management.  
With the GROW with SAP Premium package, businesses can now access SAP Concur Expense solutions and SAP Sales Cloud, providing a holistic approach to managing their operations. 

What is the new offer? 
The GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, premium Edition with material number 8014761, includes additional entitlements for:  

  • SAP Concur Expense (100 transactions per month)   
  • SAP Sales Cloud (15 users) 

In addition to the above entitlements of GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the premium package also includes many more features to accelerate development and automation for advanced financial management and procurement. 
Find more details here: https://www.sap.com/products/erp/grow/how-to-buy.html   

What is SAP Concur Expense? 

SAP Concur Expense empowers organizations to enforce spend management policies, adapt to changing requirements and regulations, and future-proof their organizations. Use SAP Concur Expense to simplify, automate and manage employee spending from receipt capture to reimbursement. Plus, with the SAP Concur mobile app, employees and supervisors can effectively manage their travel and spending from any location, on any device.  

The GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, premium package includes the following:  

  • Concur Expense including 100 expense reports per month 
  • Analytics Reporting 
  • SAP Integration with Concur between SAP Concur Expense and SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition via SAP ICS. 

How is SAP Concur Expense integrated in the offering? 

The integration of SAP Concur Expense with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is managed through the embedded financial integration module known as SAP ICS. 
This integration ensures that all expense data is fully synchronized, providing organizations with end-to-end visibility of their spending in near real-time. Here are some key benefits: 

  • Enhanced Financial Visibility: With Concur Expense, businesses can manage their employee spend quickly, simply, and accurately. The integration provides complete visibility into business expenses, allowing companies to evaluate trends and identify potential cost savings.  
  • Streamlined Operations through Automation: The seamless data synchronization between Concur Expense and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition ensures that organizations can manage their expenses efficiently. This integration eliminates the need for manual data entry, reducing errors and saving time while providing timely and accurate reimbursements.  
  • Improved Decision-Making: By leveraging the powerful analytics and reporting capabilities of Concur Expense, businesses can gain valuable insights into their spending patterns. This information can be used to make informed decisions and optimize performance.  

How is GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Premium delivered? 

The GROW with SAP Premium package is delivered as a ready-to-run service (SaaS) with pre-configured industry best practices and automated system provisioning. GROW with SAP delivers a ready-to-run SaaS ERP backed by 50 years of industry experience. When combined with Concur Expense, organizations can unlock new possibilities and drive significant growth. 

Conclusion 

The GROW with SAP Premium package, including the seamless integration between SAP Cloud ERP and SAP Concur Expense, is a game-changer for businesses looking to enhance their financial visibility and streamline their operations. By leveraging this powerful combination, organizations can stay ahead of the competition and achieve their growth objectives. 

For more information about the GROW with SAP Premium package and how it can benefit your organization, visit https://www.sap.com/products/erp/grow.html .

entry.title: SAP Integration with Concur Solutions becomes available for Concur Standard
entry.content:
Get ready for the next step in intelligent travel and expense management! Starting with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502, the SAP Integration with Concur Solutions (a.k.a. ICS a.k.a. native integration) supports a seamless integration with SAP Concur Standard Edition. This native integration offers the benefits of a streamlined integration setup by delivering the next level of automated financial integration and real-time data synchronization for growing businesses using SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

For those of you who aren't familiar yet with ICS, let me give you a short overview.

ICS is available for 

  • SAP ECC 6.0 and SAP Concur Professional Edition
  • SAP S/4HANA and SAP Concur Professional Edition
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP Concur Professional Edition
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition with SAP Concur Professional Edition and SAP Concur Standard Edition

While the scope varies for the different ERP and SAP Concur environments, you can integrate such master data like cost objects and transactional data like expense reports and cash advances.

For integrations between SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP Concur Standard Edition, consider the following:

  • Employee replication using ICS is not available. Instead, you can use the SAP Master Data Integration service to handle the replication of your employee master data in your landscape.
  • The Enhanced WBS Element Search feature is not supported.
  • The Budget Integration feature is not yet supported.
  • Processing payroll documents is not yet supported.

If you got questions/feedback, please leave a comment.

entry.title: SAP Integration with Concur Solutions: Replicating Authorized and Cost Object Approvers
entry.content:
With the S/4HANA 2023 release, the user replication of SAP Integration with Concur Solutions, the standard integration between SAP Concur and SAP S/4HANA a.k.a. ICS a.k.a. Native Integration, has been switched to the User Provisioning (UPS) v4 API. That API allows to replicate a lot more user-/employee-specific data than the previously used User v1, which has been deprecated.

Mid of 2024, the UPS API has been enhanced to support the replication of Authorized and Cost Object Approvers, a long awaited feature. With S/4HANA 2023, feature pack 3, ICS will support that feature end-2-end. I will explain how to set up a simple Cost Object Approver replication to explain the new functionality.

 

Use Case

All expenses are allocated to cost centers. The cost center responsible is supposed to approve all expenses which are allocated to their cost center.

 

How To Configure The Cost Center Approver

Prerequisites:

  • In the SAP Concur system the cost object list must be utilized for the cost object approval to make use of this functionality.
  • Maintain the employee who will be the cost center owner as such in field User Responsible (VERAK_USER) for all cost centers. The linkage between the user record and the employee record is stored in infotype 105 (Communication), sub type 0001 (System User Name), field System ID.

If you call up the field mapping of the employee replication within the transaction Concur Integration Setup (CTE_SETUP), you will see the new section spendApproverLimit. Beneath that section, you will see two more sections: authorizedApprover and costObjectApprover.

New sections for spendApproverLimit, costObjectApprover, and authorizedApprover

 

Press the Create button to the right of the costObjectApprover section. A pop-up will be raised where you need to select the approvalType. Select report for defining the costObjectApprover for an expense report.

Select report as approvalType

A data set for defining the mapping for an expense report's costObjectApprover is created. The approvalType is defaulted to report. Implementation code EE_COST_CENTER_OWNER is defaulted for determining the approvalGroup. 

Implementation Code EE_COST_CENTER_OWNER is defaulted

The implementation code EE_COST_CENTER_OWNER works as follows:

  1. Determine all cost centers where the employee who is supposed to be replicated is maintained as User Responsible (VERAK_USER).
  2. Add a data record to assign the employee as cost center approver to each cost center from Step 1. In the payload that is sent to Concur, you will see the approvalGroup field filled according to the definition for the cost center replication.
    Let's assume that you use the set up which is considered as Best Practice, i.e., Logical System -> Company Code -> Cost Object Type -> Cost Object ID. Then the value in the approvalGroup field will be the concatenation of those 4 values separated by a dash, e.g. QVGCLNT354-1710-CC-SE_301024.

Depending on your Concur set up, you might also have to provide values for the other fields in the costObjectApprover section, e.g. approvalLimit, reimbursementCurrency, level ...

 

What's Missing

So far, we've defined that an employee can be replicated as cost center approver for expense reports. But we haven't defined yet that the employee should also get the related authorization (role Expense Approver, EXP_APPROVER).
The role assignment is done via the section spendRole. If you haven't defined a mapping for the Expense Approver role yet, press the Create button for spendRole and select EXP_APPROVER from the pop-up. 

 

Select Expense Approver role

Now, you can define when the Expense Approver role should be assigned to an employee. To assign the role to an employee that is maintained as User Responsible for a cost center (see also the explanation above for EE_COST_CENTER_OWNER),  pick the implementation code EE_IS_CC_RESPONSIBLE for the field roleName.

Enter EE_IS_CC_RESPONSIBLE as implementation code for the field roleName

Save your changes. From now on, the system will replicate an employee as cost object approver if the employee is the User Responsible for at least 1 cost center.

 

Additional Remarks

  • Authorized approvers are configured in a similar manner as cost object approvers.
  • For determining whether an employee should be assigned an approver role, the implementation code EE_IS_RLU_MGR_OR_CC_RESP will also be available. The implementation code combines the functionalities of EE_IS_RLU_MANAGER and  EE_IS_CC_RESPONSIBLE.
  • The BAdI BADI_CTE_HCM_EE_DATA_CHANGE_V4 has been enhanced to cover spendApproverLimit functionality. For example, you can create your own logic to determine the approvalGroup by implementing the BAdI.

entry.title: Are you an administrator and need an introduction to configuration tools of Concur Expense Standard?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Getting Started with Concur Expense Standard for Administrators learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey is an introduction to Concur Expense Standard for Administrators and Functional Consultants. In this comprehensive journey, you will learn the configuration tools and components of Concur Expense Standard, from the Basic and Core Configuration, to Payments, and finally the optional configurations.

Learning objectives
At the end of this learning journey, administrators and functional consultants will be able to,  in Concur Expense Standard:

  • Navigate the Expense Settings page
  • Configure the tools and components
  • Process payments and extracts
  • Navigate reporting 

Prerequisites
Learners should have a firm understanding of how Concur Expense operates as a business user.
Please complete the Getting Started with Concur Expense for Customer Users learning journey before completing this course. 

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Handling Cash Advances with SAP Concur's Financial Integration Service v4 API's
entry.content:
I'm a long-time Technical Consultant at SAP Concur, specializing in data interfaces between SAP Concur and its customers. In this article, I'll walk you through using SAP Concur's Financial Integration Service (FIS) v4 Web Service API to manage the issuance and repayment of cash advances.

Prerequisites

Before diving in, it's essential to have a basic understanding of the FIS API. If you're not familiar, I highly recommend reading this blog post.   You'll also need a Concur Web application with the following scope:

  • FISVC

Introduction

Cash advances can be incredibly useful for travelers. Here’s why:

  • Convenience: They provide quick access to local currency, especially when you're short on cash or in a place without accessible banking facilities. This feature is a lifesaver in emergencies or remote locations.
  • Reduced Need to Carry Large Amounts of Cash: Carrying less cash reduces the risk of loss or theft, enhancing your personal safety.
  • Emergency Situations: A cash advance can act as a financial lifeline when unexpected expenses arise, such as medical emergencies or hotel bills.
  • Flexibility: You can withdraw money anytime, regardless of local bank hours, which is highly convenient in countries with different banking schedules.
  • No Need for Prior Currency Exchange: Avoid the hassle and expense of currency exchange services. Cash advances often secure better exchange rates compared to traditional methods.

However, please keep in mind that cash advances usually come with fees and interest charges, often higher than typical credit card purchases. Use them judiciously to manage your travel budget effectively

How SAP Concur Supports Cash Advances

SAP Concur primarily facilitates cash advances in two ways:

  • Employee-Initiated Requests: Employees can request cash advances before they travel using SAP Concur’s Cash Advance Request feature.  If you would like to see a process flow describing a standard process, click here.
  • Card-Initiated Transactions: Transactions that come directly from your corporate card issuer.

Both methods have financial posting implications and tracking requirements, making the Financial Integration Service APIs invaluable.

Remember, there are 3 steps to Financial Integration Service:

  1. Getting Financial Transactions
  2. Acknowledging each Financial Transaction
  3. Confirming each Financial Transaction (success or fail)

For issuing a cash advance, use doctype=cashadvance. For repayment and tracking, use doctype=expense. 

Detailed Walkthrough

Issuing a Cash Advance

Let's focus on the critical data elements rather than the actual API calls, which are detailed in the original FIS blog post.  When receiving a document:

  • “id”: This is the docId used for acknowledgements and posting confirmations.
  • “employeeData”: Identifies the employee receiving the cash advance.
  • “cardTransactionID”: If provided, helps reconcile the advance to the corporate card.
  • “cashAdvanceId”: Use this field to track the cash advance.
  • “journalData”: Tells you whether to pay the employee or credit the card.

Here's an example of a cash advance taken out on a corporate card:

In the above example, the employee used their CBCP corporate card to take out a cash advance for $26.07. The accounting entries would be to debit the "CASHADV" account and credit the "card clearing account" for 26.07.

For employee-initiated cash advances, the cardTransaction* fields are null and the journalData looks like:

The accounting would debit the "CASHADV from empl" account and credit the employees payable for 1000.00.

Please remember that the cash advance must be acknowledged and posting confirmed successful before the employee can submit expenses against it. Otherwise, it will be in Pending or Failure status and can only be accessed by your Concur Cash Advance Administrator. 

Repayment of a Cash Advance

After the cash advance is confirmed, employees will associate the advance with an expense report and submit expenses against it. These expenses will all be cash/out-of-pocket expenses.

In the new section called "cashAdvanceApplication" within the JSON from the GET call, each cash expenseEntry that was used to repay the advance will be listed.

Here's an example:

In the above JSON, two accounting entries are needed:

  1. From the journal section: debit account "Dinnerx" and credit employee payable for 26.07.
  2. From the cashAdvanceApplication section: debit employee payable and credit "CASHADV".

Concur ensures that the employee repays the advance. Depending on the configuration, repayment can be completed in one or multiple expense reports. More than one advance can be associated with a single expense report, necessitating multiple entries in the cashAdvanceApplication section for each cashAdvanceID..

Conclusion

Cash advances can be complex to manage, but with SAP Concur’s Financial Integration Service, you can issue and track them effortlessly. The near real-time bi-directional interface provides critical feedback for the ERP posting process.

With the insights from this post and the Postman collection from the previous blog, you should be well on your way to exploring the benefits of the Financial Integration Service.

Share and Connect

What do you think?  Do you have anything to add? Leave a comment below.

Did you find it useful? Give us a like and share on social media.

Do you have questions or want to know more about SAP Concur and its offerings? Please follow us here.

Thank you!

entry.title: A Place for Learning and Collaboration
entry.content:
The SAP Community is a global platform where professionals, developers, and learners come together to share knowledge, ask questions, and stay updated on the latest trends in SAP technologies. Whether you're a beginner or an experienced SAP consultant, this community offers valuable resources, including blogs, discussion forums, and expert advice.

One of the best features of the SAP Community is its interactive Q&A section, where users can post queries and get solutions from SAP experts and fellow members. Additionally, the platform provides access to tutorials, webinars, and technical documentation, making it an excellent place for continuous learning.

By participating in discussions, contributing blogs, and networking with industry professionals, members can enhance their SAP skills and stay ahead in their careers. If you're working with SAP or looking to start, joining the SAP Community can be a great step in your learning journey! 😊

entry.title: An Overview at Concur Mobile Security
entry.content:
In this time, where mobility is essential. As more employees work remotely and travel for business, finding secure mobile solutions has never been more critical. SAP Concur offers a robust mobile app that meets the needs of today’s customers. Concur Mobile not only simplifies expense tracking and travel management but also prioritizes security to protect sensitive data. 

Why Mobile Security Matters 

Mobile devices have become a crucial part of business operations, but they also come with unique security challenges. From lost or stolen devices to unsecured networks, mobile environments are highly susceptible to cyber threats. SAP Concur tackles these challenges with a comprehensive suite of security features to safeguard data and ensure compliance. 

Key Features of Concur Mobile Security 

To ensure High-level security, SAP Concur Mobile requires all users to protect their devices with a passcode. The app is designed not to function on jailbroken or rooted devices. If your device is compromised in this way, Concur Mobile will notify you, and you will need to perform a factory reset on your device to continue using the application. This added layer of security helps keep your sensitive information safe. here are some of the key features that SAP Concur app offers:

 1. Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) 

A key element of Concur Mobile security is multi-factor authentication (MFA). By requiring users to provide multiple forms of identification, MFA significantly reduces the risk of unauthorized access. This additional security means that even if login credentials are compromised, they alone are not enough to gain access to sensitive information. 

 2. Biometric Authentication 

Concur Mobile also supports biometric authentication methods such as fingerprint and facial recognition. Biometric authentication adds an extra layer of security by ensuring that only the authorized user can access the app. This method is not only more secure but also more convenient, reducing the need for complex passwords and multiple login steps. Biometric login is turned on by default for SAP Concur Mobile. If you need to make adjustments or customize this feature, simply submit a request to SAP Concur Support 

 3. Data Encryption 

Data encryption is a critical component of Concur Mobile security. Here’s a detailed look at how SAP Concur uses encryption to protect your data: 

 

  • End-to-End Encryption:  SAP Concur uses Transport Layer Security (TLS) and Secure Sockets Layer (SSL) protocols to encrypt data during transmission. This ensures that any data exchanged between the mobile app and SAP Concur's servers is protected from unauthorized interception. 
  • Encryption at Rest: SAP Concur uses Advanced Encryption Standard (AES-256) to encrypt data stored on devices and in the cloud. AES-256 is one of the strongest encryption algorithms available, ensuring that even if a device or server is compromised, the data remains secure. 
  • High Security Account (HSA) :Some customers have high-security requirements that dictate encryption keys must not be stored on the device itself. To meet these needs, company admins can enable the High Security Account (HSA) setting for their accounts. When HSA is enabled, users must create an additional application password that decrypts their data in memory. This extra layer of security means that every time you do a cold start of the app, you'll need to enter this password. It’s an extra step, but it ensures that your data remains protected at all times. 

 4. Single Sign-On (SSO) for Mobile 

Single Sign-On (SSO) is another critical security feature that enhances the user experience while maintaining robust security. SSO allows users to log in once and access multiple applications, reducing the need for multiple logins and passwords. With SAP Concur, SSO is supported on mobile devices, making it easier for employees to manage their travel and expenses securely. 

 5. Mobile Device Management (MDM) Support 

SAP Concur supports Mobile Device Management (MDM) solutions. MDM enables IT administrators to manage and secure mobile devices remotely, ensuring that corporate data is protected at all times. 

 6. Account Lockout Mechanisms  

SAP Concur has implemented a lockout feature for both the web version and the mobile app. If you enter an incorrect username, password, or SSO too many times in a row (as set by your company's administrator), your password or SSO credentials will be cleared. To regain access, you’ll need to follow your company’s specific process for unlocking your account. This lockout mechanism helps protect your account from unauthorized access, ensuring your data remains secure. 

 7. Regular Updates and Patch Management 

Keeping mobile applications up-to-date is crucial for maintaining security. SAP Concur regularly updates the Concur Mobile app to address vulnerabilities and enhance security features. Automatic updates ensure users are always protected by the latest security measures. 

 8. Ensuring Compliance  

SAP Concur Mobile is a key part of the SAP Concur suite and is designed to meet rigorous security standards. These include SAP’s Cloud Services Security Framework, PCI, GDPR, and ISO 27001 certifications. To keep everything secure, the platform goes through annual vulnerability assessments that evaluate both the mobile app and mobile web services. This robust compliance framework ensures your data stays safe and aligns with strict regulatory requirements. 

 9. Integrating Security into Development Practices 

SAP Concur also ensures security at the code level by proactively maintaining up-to-date dependencies and mitigating risks. Automated tools analyze potential vulnerabilities whenever dependencies are updated, ensuring third-party components remain secure. Additionally, static code analysis enforces consistent coding standards while helping to identify and address vulnerabilities in early stages of the development cycle. 

Conclusion

In the present day, having a secure mobile solution is a necessity. SAP Concur’s mobile app is designed to provide businesses with the tools they need to manage travel, expenses, and invoices effectively. By prioritizing mobile security, including robust encryption, biometric authentication, SSO capabilities, MDM support, and comprehensive data protection, SAP Concur helps businesses protect their data while complying with regulations. This makes it an invaluable tool.

entry.title: WalkMe – More than a walk through
entry.content:
It's common knowledge by now that the Concur User Assistant (CUA) is powered by WalkMe and that there are two versions of the CUA - Standard and Custom

Standard content that has been pre-built by SAP Concur that your users can access via Explore Interactive Help from the Help menu or other on-screen prompts.

 

 

Custom built content which has been built specifically for your entity either internally, through a Partner, or with an SAP Concur project team.

A lot of the time it can be viewed that the CUA only provides Smart Walk Throughs (SWT) where users are guided through a particular process through step-by-step on-screen prompts, or with Smart Tips where users can hover over an icon and can view further details.

WalkMe has more potential than this. A lot more.

 

The example that I am going to focus on in this article is one that has come up on a few projects - Users entering credit card expenses as cash expenses.

You know the scenario - a user may not always have the patience to wait for the credit card provider to process the expense, for it to be sent to Concur and then released to the user to add to a claim/report (*). This can lead to a reconciliation irregularity and the need for the accounts/finance team to perform reversals etc. to bring everything back into line.

 (*) I use UK English, where an Expense Report is more commonly known as an Expense Claim

I'll take you through the steps on how you can modify the user’s behavior when building your own custom content.

Step 1

Create a Launcher that will sit on top of the Add Expense button inside a report.

 

 

This will end up becoming an invisible Launcher, so that users will not actually realize they are clicking on it to begin with. But, for the purpose of building and testing it is best left visible so we can see what we are doing with it.

 

Step 2

Create an Automatic Smart Walk Through that will always take the user to the "Available Expenses" tab of the Add Expense to Report popup. What can happen is when a user clicks on the Add Expense button, the pop-up defaults to the last used setting, which can sometimes be the "New Expense" tab; Users then just go on to create the new expense rather than taking a look at what they have available.

 

The aim here will be to mimic Concur's normal behavior - the SWT will need to have two Auto-Steps

  1. Clicking the "Add Expense" button on behalf of the user.
  2. Clicking on the "Available Expenses" tab

 

Step 3

Connect the newly created SWT to the Launcher

 The overall flow will look like this:

User clicks on invisible launcher -> Launcher triggers SWT -> SWT clicks on Add Expense -> SWT clicks on Available Expenses

 

Step 4

Once you are happy with the testing, you can then make the Launcher invisible by selecting Edit on Page:

 

And then selecting the ghost icon:

Don’t forget to Save!

You can, of course, take it a further as the above method doesn't stop uses navigating to the New Expense tab. This is where you can in components to actively warn users against making cash expense entries where they should use what is in their Available Expenses.

Step 5

Here you can employ another invisible Launcher on the New Expense tab.

As before, it remains visible so you can see it for testing and building out the solution.

 

Step 6

This Launcher will kick off another Smart Walk Through - This time however you can start with a popup for the user to warn them.

 

 

Step 7

Add an auto-step to click on the New Expense tab for the users who are making a genuine cash expense entry.

 

As before, once you are happy, don't forget to make the New Expenses Launcher invisible!

 

Every time a user then clicks on, what they believe to be the New Expense tab, it will kick off this SWT and they will receive the warning that you have written.

Of course, the above is just an example - the wording that you can use is very much up to you.

 

Overall, this is just a small sample of what you can achieve with a customized WalkMe solution.

entry.title: Are you looking for learning materials for the Travel & Expense solution area?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Discovering Travel & Expense Solutions learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey is designed for you to discover the Travel & Expense solution area. 

Learning objectives
Upon completing this learning journey, you will be able to understand the fundamental aspects of the Travel & Expense solutions and their potential. You will learn about SAP’s cloud strategy, the integrated portfolio, and how it connects to end-to-end business processes and industries.

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Be Part of Tomorrow: Join SAP's Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 1, 2025!
entry.content:
We’re thrilled to announce the launch of the first cycle of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative for 2025, featuring 50 exciting new projects aimed at fostering innovation and collaboration with our valued SAP customers and partners.

In today’s fast-paced business world, staying ahead is more important than ever. The SAP Customer Engagement Initiative presents a unique opportunity to gain early insights into SAP’s product developments. By participating, you’ll work directly with our developers and product managers, helping shape the future of our products.

Register until March 28, 2025! Don’t miss out on the opportunity to have your voice heard and influence the next generation of SAP solutions.

To help you explore the available projects, we’ve compiled a detailed list for your convenience. Check out the full list here or follow the links below to discover the exciting opportunities:

ABAP

  • ABAP Cloud Development Assistant
  • OData UI Service from scratch Generator

SAP Business Technology Platform

  • BTP AI based Data Anonymization Service
  • Create AI-driven extensions and automations with SAP Build
  • Improving the Admin's and Solution Architect's Experience on SAP BTP
  • SAP BPM, SAP BRM and SAP Process Orchestration migration to Cloud
  • SAP BTP Cockpit, Cloud Foundry UIs
  • SAP BTP Usability Event - User research project
  • SAP Business Technology Platform: Multi-Region Desaster Recovery
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Provisioning, Integration with Joule 
  • SAP Integration Suite

SAP Concur

  • SAP Concur - AI Managed Expense Reports
  • SAP Concur - Dynamic Card Management – Virtual Credit Cards
  • SAP Concur Expense - Web Services for Financial Integration
  • SAP Concur Mobile app and the sign-in user experience
  • SAP Concur Solutions – All About E-Receipts
  • SAP Concur Solutions - Customer Managed Keys for granular control over data
  • SAP Concur Solutions - Sponsored Guest Feature
  • SAP Concur Solutions - Web Notifications Center and Preferences

SAP Customer Experience

  • Research project into a potential loyalty cloud solution
  • SAP CX AI Toolkit

Data & Analytics

  • Redesigning the Dashboard Experience
  • SAP HANA Cloud Elastic Compute Node
  • SAP HANA Database Migration to SAP HANA Cloud

SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  • Bank Relationship Management: Improving Overview, Services & Fee, and Interest Calculations
  • Fiori First for Sales Order Management
  • Identifying and resolving critical situations in a production planners day in S/4HANA Public Cloud
  • Identity and Access Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Insights into Business processes with Business Event Logging
  • Joule and GenAI use cases in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud
  • New Financial Reporting for Enterprise Project (Project Booklet) including ETO
  • Planned New Solution for Cost Component Breakdown
  • Project staffing, consider dependencies across different roles in a project
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - Product Change Management
  • Simplification of transports in SAP S/4HANA® Cloud Public Edition, three-system landscape
  • Usability Improvements for managing attachments in S/4HANA business applications

SAP Globalization

  • Australia and New Zealand: GST requirement for Down payments
  • Australia: Modified Payment Times Report - AASB 8 and Taxable Payments Annual Report
  • ISO 21378 Audit Data Collection Standard
  • Planned enhancements to Run statutory reports to handle Draft Report reconciliation
  • Sales and Use Tax Reporting in United States - User Research
  • Sales and Use Tax payments in United States

Lifecycle Management

  • DevOps with ABAP

SAP Integration Experience Feedback goes SAP Customer Engagement Initiative

  • Assisted Assessment of Aligned Business APIs for External Workforce
  • Assisted Assessment of Aligned Business APIs for Hire to Retire Cloud
  • Assisted Assessment of Aligned Business APIs for Source-to-Pay

Others

  • Research into AI-Assisted User Learning Rollout for Adoption
  • SAP Reference Architecture in SAP LeanIX
  • Shape the future of SAP Cloud ALM Configuration & Security Analysis
  • The AI-Driven Future of New Product Introduction (NPI)

We believe that collaboration is key to sparking innovation. By working alongside our customers and partners, we can jointly shape the future of SAP’s products and transform business operations.

Let’s take this journey together. Together, we have the power to drive progress and transform industries.

Don’t miss out – sign up today at influence.sap.com and be a part of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 1 in 2025!

Warm regards, 
Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team 

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: SAP Concur user authentication to concursolutions.com
entry.content:
I have been a Technical Consultant at SAP Concur for many years and I understand how important security is in our digital world today. Security has always been a crucial element throughout the entire product lifecycle of all SAP products, including product development, planning, and quality assurance.

In this post, I will explain the three authentication methods that can be used by end users to log in to concursolutions.com.

Introduction

 

Security aspects that may require business decisions are managed by the customers of SAP Concur. A typical example of this is user and authorization management.

There are three different methods that users of SAP Concur can use to authenticate. In this blog post, I will provide a summary of the three different authentication methods, which can be defined as follows:

  1. Sign in with SAP Concur password
  2. Sign in with Email link (Passwordless Sign-in)
  3. Sign in with Single Sign-on

 

Detailed Walkthrough

 

Sign in with SAP Concur password 

This method of authentication is represented by logging in on concursolutions.com via the username and password that are stored in SAP Concur.The password authentication is one of the most commonly used login methods. Therefore, it is important to educate users about the risks associated with weak passwords.
Users of SAP Concur can sign in to their personal profiles by using the password stored within SAP Concur. There are few points that I strongly recommend:

  • Do not share your password with anyone. SAP Concur Support will never ask you to disclose your profile password.
  • Use a unique and complex password for each account to prevent unauthorized access if one gets compromised.
  • Be vigilant of phishing email messages that asks you to click on links of provide sensitive information. For more information, refer to the Phishing topic.

The user with the company administrator role for your SAP Concur system can define the password policy on the Sign-In Settings page.


Accessing Sign-In Settings

To access the Sign-In Settings page the company administrator clicks on the Home button -> Authentication Admin.

On the Authentication Administration page, click Sign-In Settings.


This page allows administrators to configure the following requirements and parameters:

  • Password strength - There you can define the minimum length of passwords that users must use. Nowadays, passwords with 12 to 14 characters, including uppercase and lowercase letters, numbers, and special characters such as #, %, and @, are considered safe.

 

  • Password change - In this section, you can set how long a user can use the same password and how often a user can change their password. The password expiration feature must be strictly applied to align with the 90-day password expiration policy recommended by SAP Concur.

 

  • Account Lockout - This is a feature that specifies after how many failed password attempts, within a certain timeframe, the user will be locked out. The lockout will persist for a specified period or until the password is reset by either the user or the administrator if it is set to 'permanent lockout'. This feature provides a robust defense against brute force techniques, which rely on guessing the correct password.

 

  • Session Timeout - This feature defines the idle time before automatic user sign-out from SAP Concur. For example, if you log in to concursolutions.com to perform an action and then go for lunch while still having the SAP Concur session open in your browser, it will enter an idle state. After 30 minutes of inactivity, while you are having lunch, you will be logged out from the website.

 

  • Other Settings - Here you will find some additional policy settings. You can restrict the username from being sent to the user's email if it is forgotten. Additionally, you can require the user to change their initial password upon their first login.

The third option is related to the Two-Factor Authentication and the requirement for the user to receive an activation email link. Enabling this option is strongly recommended, as it adds an extra layer of security to user accounts in your SAP Concur system.

The fourth option involves the enablement of the passwordless sign-in feature, which will be covered in the section below: 'Sign in with Email Link (Passwordless Sign-in).' 

 

The authentication method to Sign in with SAP Concur password requires a two-factor authentication (2FA) to be setup and used.

When an SAP Concur user signs in using their username and password for the first time, they will be prompted to set up 2FA. During the setup process, the user will receive an email from SAP Concur with further instructions on how to configure 2FA.

The user must then use a third-party authenticator mobile application to scan a QR code or manually enter a key during the 2FA enrollment process.

Once 2FA is set up, users signing in to SAP Concur with a username and password must use 2FA during the sign-in process.

This means users will be prompted to enter a one-time 6-digit code generated by an authenticator mobile application each time they sign in to SAP Concur. For more information on the 2FA feature, you can read more here. - https://dam.sap.com/mac/app/p/pdf/asset/preview/FrhUmfQ?ltr=a&rc=10

 

Sign in with Email link (Passwordless Sign-in)

This feature is available only for the web-based version of SAP Concur. It is not available for the mobile-based login or the mobile application of SAP Concur.

By using it, the user can receive an email with a one-time link to sign in. The user should have a valid email address configured in their profile. This method of authentication provides a smoother and more efficient user experience without compromising security.

The one-time link has an expiry time of 1 hour and is sent only to the user.

The security aspect relies on the user's email account, which needs to be well-maintained and secured to prevent compromise. Passwordless sign-in is enabled by default for clients who have Single Sign-On set as optional. Company administrators can enable or disable the feature in the Sign-In Settings.

More about this feature can be found here - https://dam.sap.com/mac/app/p/pdf/asset/preview/z7oNDZn?ltr=a&rc=10&doi=SAP1114633

 

 Sign in with Single Sign-on

 The third option for user authentication that SAP Concur supports is Single Sign-On (SSO) via the SAML 2.0 standard.

SAML SSO functionality involves two parties: an Identity Provider (IdP) and a Service Provider (SP). In this situation, SAP Concur is the service provider.

SAP Concur supports any identity providers that comply with the SAML 2.0 standard. SAP Concur supports both IdP-Initiated and SP-Initiated SSO.

The IdP-Initiated SSO is explained with the following scenario: users sign in to the identity provider and then click on a link or tile on the IdP page to access SAP Concur.

With SP-Initiated SSO, the user navigates to concursolutions.com, enters their username, and selects the appropriate SSO option. After users successfully authenticate with the identity provider, an active session of SAP Concur will be opened. The SP-Initiated authentication flow is used by the mobile application of SAP Concur.

The SAP Concur user that has the permission called 'SSO admin' will have access to the Single Sign-On Self Service tool, where customer can manage their own SSO configuration.

From the SSO self-service tool the SSO administrators can obtain the SAP Concur SP metadata that needs to be uploaded into the customer's identity provider. After that the IdP metadata file needs to be generated from the customer's identity provider and uploaded to SAP Concur.

To access the SSO self-service tool the company administrator clicks on the Home button -> Authentication Admin. On the Authentication Administration page, click on Manage Single Sign-on.

The SSO admin of the company can set the SSO sign-in policy on the Manage Single Sign-on page.

 

By default, the SSO sign-in policy is set to "SSO Optional." This setting ensures that all three different methods of authentication in SAP Concur are enabled: "Sign in with SAP Concur password," "Sign in with email link (passwordless sign-in)," and "Sign in with single sign-on." 

"If the SSO sign-in policy is changed to 'SSO Required,' all users will need to sign in to SAP Concur through an identity provider using SSO."

Be cautious when enabling the SSO Required policy because all users in your SAP Concur system—including TMCs, administrators, web services, and test user accounts—will be blocked from signing into concursolutions.com with their username and password stored in SAP Concur.

Currently, the SSO sign-in policy can only be applied at the company level.

In the ‘Manage Single Sign-On’ page, customers can upload an unlimited number of IdP metadata files. This allows customers to connect an unlimited number of IdP applications to a single SAP Concur entity. The SSO administrators can assign different names to each configured IdP, such as ‘Okta (Company A).’

This helps users know which one to choose during SP-initiated SSO sign-in.

 

While configuring an IdP in Managed Single Sign-On, the SSO administrators can choose to hide the SSO option from the login page of SAP Concur. Enabling this setting is suitable if you are performing SSO testing.

 

The SSO admin can specify a logout URL that will redirect the user to a specific page after signing out of SAP Concur.

Here, you will find more resources about the SSO setup in SAP Concur - https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_CONCUR_SECURITY/b92b8c7fc75a4c8faf62a6584077b022/8bfaad63987d455183bf7cdcd75c578a.html

Conclusion


After reading this post, you will know more about the three authentication methods for accessing concursolutions.com and be able to choose the right combination for your needs.

 

Share and Connect


What do you think?  Do you have anything to add? Leave a comment below.
Did you find it useful? Give us a like and share on social media.
Want to know more about SAP Concur? Please follow here.
Want to ask questions about SAP Concur and its offerings? Ask here
Follow my profile for similar content.

Thank you!

entry.title: SAP Concur - Creating Invoice Purchase Requests via API
entry.content:
This is the second part of a series on utilizing SAP Concur Invoice APIs. Part one deals with vendor management and can be found here. This post will focus on creating purchase requests.

Prerequisites

You must be contracted for Invoice Processing, Purchase Requests for Invoice, and Client Web Services.

This post will not discuss authentication. Please see this post for an introduction to authentication and web service application building within Concur. Please use this link to download a Postman collection for the Invoice APIs. The complete documentation for the Purchase Request v4 API can be found on the SAP Concur Developer Center. Note that this collection assumes you’re using the environment from the authentication blog linked above. You will need an application with the following scopes:

  • purchaserequest.read
  • purchaserequest.write
  • INVPMT (needed to get the expense type codes sent in the purchase request payload)

Works with these SAP Concur solutions:

  • Invoice – Standard
  • Invoice – Professional

Background

Before we get into the nuts and bolts of the APIs, here’s a quick primer on the lifecycle of the requisition to payment process:

  1. An employee needs to buy a good or service. Before ordering / signing a contract, the employee must first seek approval to ensure the purchase is appropriate and is within budget. A purchase request is created, which contains a list of requested items, the quantity for each, and the unit price.
  2. The purchase request is submitted and will go through an approval workflow as designed by the customer. This could be a simple 1-up approval to the manager, or a more complex approval that goes to a cost approver depending on a cost center, project, or other cost object that was coded on the request.
  3. Once the purchase request is approved, a new purchase order is created based on the request details and is submitted to the vendor.
  4. The vendor receives the purchase order and ships goods / delivers services as needed.
  5. The vendor generates an invoice that’s sent to the customer. Note that you may also see invoices referred to as Payment Requests in some Concur documentation.
  6. The goods / services are received by the customer, and a goods receipt may be created indicating the quantity and date of receipt of the goods received.
  7. Accounts payable receives the invoice and matches it against the PO that was sent as well as the receipt—this is known as 3-way matching. Any discrepancies are noted and adjustments to the invoice and/or the PO  are made as needed.
  8. The invoice itself goes through an approval process. If the invoice matches the PO it can be configured to skip approval steps. If there were discrepancies between the invoice and the PO, then additional approvals can be required.
  9. Once the invoice is fully approved, it is released for financial posting and payment. Payment can be made directly from Concur via Invoice Pay or can be handled in an external system.
  10. Once payment has been confirmed, the payment status within Concur can be updated as appropriate.

SAP Concur Invoice has functionality to handle all these steps, but there are some scenarios where it may make sense to handle part of the process outside of Concur and use APIs to push the data to Concur instead.

Let’s say you already have an ordering system with a robust punch-out catalog, but maybe the approval functionality is limited and inconvenient for end users to use. In this case it makes sense to continue handling the order request creation process in your external system, but send the purchase request to Concur so you can take advantage of the flexible and easy-to-use approval process offered by Concur. In this scenario, step 1 from above will be completed by the purchase request API, and steps 2-10 will be handled through the normal Concur process.

Create a Purchase Request

Creating a purchase request is quite straightforward. The payload is separated into two parts: the header information that applies to the request as a whole, and one or more line items that details exactly what is being requested. Within the Postman collection, expand the Purchase Request folder and click on the Create Purchase Request row:

Line items do not all need to share the same vendor code or vendor address. If multiple vendors are used, note that a separate PO will be created for each distinct vendor code and vendor address combination.

The exact details that are required to be populated will vary based on your site’s configuration, but the payload in the sample Postman collection is typical of what’s needed. The complete schema details are here. Some of the values needed are also dependent on your configuration – here’s a list of those fields and where in the configuration you can find the configured values (you may need to work with one of your Concur admins and/or your Concur implementation team if you don’t have admin rights):

  • policyExternalId: Administration -> Invoice -> Policies – open the desired policy and scroll to the bottom:
  • shipToAddressCode and billToAddressCode: Administration -> Invoice -> Company Locations – use the value in the Address Code column:
  • vendorCode and vendorAddressCode: see my previous blog on vendor management.
  • expenseType: use the “Get Codes for Localized Strings” request in the Postman collection:
  • uomCode: Administration -> Invoice -> Units of Measure – Use the value in the Code column:

Once you have your payload built, you can send the POST request to create the purchase request. The response for a successfully created purchase request looks like this:

You can then make a GET request to the returned URI to see some basic details about the request:

 

Note that the approval status is “Pending Approval”. Purchase requests created via API are automatically submitted unless there’s a blocking exception or there’s no default approver assigned. In that case, the user will need to manually submit the request after clearing the exceptions or working with HR to have an approver assigned as needed.

At this stage, no further interaction with the request via API is possible. The Purchase Request endpoint does not support the PATCH / PUT / DELETE methods so you cannot make changes to a purchase request once it’s been created. When the purchase request workflow is completed, a new purchase order is created based on the details of the request and is transmitted to the appropriate vendor(s).

At this point, you can retrieve the IDs of the created purchase order(s) by making a GET request to the purchase request endpoint again:

You would then be able to use the PO numbers to call the purchase order endpoint in order to retrieve the PO details. I will cover those APIs in part three of this series, so stay tuned!

Share and Connect

What do you think?  Do you have anything to add? Leave a comment below.
Did you find it useful? Give us a like and share on social media.
Want to know more about SAP Concur? Please follow here.
Want to ask questions about SAP Concur and its offerings? Ask here.

Thank you!

entry.title: Your opportunity to Influence SAP: SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Projects 2025-1 for SAP Concur
entry.content:
Connect financial data and take control of spending. By automating spending processes your business can run efficiently anywhere, anytime, and under any circumstances.

Scroll through the list of SAP Customer Engagement Initiative projects for SAP Concur and get informed about the planned new features and functions.

And don’t forget to register for one of the projects until March 28, 2025

SAP Concur Solutions projects for registration:

 

SAP Concur Solutions - Customer Managed Keys for granular control over data

SAP Concur - Dynamic Card Management – Virtual Credit Cards

SAP Concur Solutions - Web Notifications Center and Preferences

SAP Concur Mobile app and the sign-in user experience

SAP Concur Solutions – All About E-Receipts

SAP Concur Solutions - Sponsored Guest Feature

SAP Concur - AI Managed Expense Reports

SAP Concur Expense - Web Services for Financial Integration

 

Visit the Customer Influence website on influence.sap.com to register.

If you are also interested in other CEI projects that are currently open for registration, see here the full list.

Thank you in advance for your participation!

 

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.
